correct_explanation: long-scoreboard stalls dominate because the kernel spends most of its time waiting on dependent operations rather than saturating bandwidth
raw_response:
ASSESSMENT: DISAGREE

REASONING: The primary bottleneck is register pressure leading to low occupancy, likely with possible local-memory traffic if the compiler spills `taps[32]`, `state[96]`, and `partial[32]`; this kernel keeps a very large per-thread working set and uses `__launch_bounds__(256,1)`, which further constrains residency. Although there are many dependent FMAs/adds, the kernel is dominated more by occupancy-limited execution (and potentially register-spill effects) than by classic long-scoreboard waiting on global memory, since most arithmetic operates on thread-local arrays after the initial loads.

Although there are many dependent FMAs/adds, the kernel is dominated more by occupancy-limited execution (and potentially register-spill effects) than by classic long-scoreboard waiting on global memory, since most arithmetic operates on thread-local arrays after the initial loads.
